short.title: Short introduction
short.description: 10 core questions, about 2 minutes. A lighter first step.

long.title: Deeper introduction
long.description: 18 core questions, about 5 minutes. More nuance, more precise match.

answers.a: Daily life or work is weighing on me, my energy is depleted
answers.b: There's one specific thing I'd like to talk about
answers.c: I'm going through a big change and need support
answers.d: I can't name it yet — it just feels unclear and heavy

pq1.text: How long has this been weighing on you?

answers.a: Quite recently — it still feels new
answers.c: For a longer time, over a year
answers.d: For as long as I can remember
My identity or sense of self

q1b.otherPlaceholder: Tell us in your own words...

q1c.text: Does any of these feel close to what you're experiencing?
q1c.infoText: Optional — you can skip this if you prefer.

pq2.text: Which area of life would you like to focus on?
pq2.infoText: You can choose more than one.

answers.e: Something else — I'll describe it later

pq3.text: How much is this affecting your daily life right now?
pq3.note: If you feel you're in crisis, the fastest available support is always the most important next step.

answers.a: I'm managing, but wanted to seek support early
answers.b: Getting by, but my energy is low
answers.c: It feels quite heavy and I need help now

q2.text: What do you want from therapy right now?

answers.b: I want to understand myself better
answers.c: I want to solve a specific problem
answers.d: I want to create real change

q3.text: What kind of person helps you most in difficult moments?

q4.text: How would you like your therapist to work with you?

q5.text: What kind of working style feels natural to you?

answers.a: Relaxed and informal — no rigid structure
answers.b: Clear and structured — I like knowing where we stand

pq4.text: How do you usually deal with difficult things?

answers.a: I talk to people close to me — connection helps
answers.b: I reflect quietly on my own
answers.c: I do something concrete — action helps
answers.d: It varies depending on the situation

pq5.text: What pace would feel right for you?

answers.a: Gently, at my own pace — no rush
answers.b: Goal-oriented — I want to feel progress

q6.text: How important is your therapist's experience to you?

answers.a: Not crucial — what matters most is the right chemistry
answers.b: A few years of experience is enough
answers.d: Life experience matters a great deal to me

q7.text: Does the gender of your therapist matter to you?

q8.text: What price range feels realistic per session?

answers.d: Price is not a deciding factor

q9.text: Would you like to use Kela reimbursement if possible?
q9.note: Short-term therapy is not covered by Kela.

answers.a: Yes, show me only eligible therapists
answers.b: Would be nice but not essential

pq6.text: How often were you thinking of going?

answers.a: Once a week — I want to progress actively
answers.b: A couple of times a month
answers.d: I'm not sure yet — I'll try and see

q10.text: Would you prefer to meet remotely or in person?

answers.a: Remotely — video works well for me
answers.b: In person — physical presence matters to me

q10b.text: In which city or town would you like to meet?
q10b.infoText: Optional — you can skip this if you prefer.

pq7.text: Do you have any previous experience with therapy or similar support?

answers.a: No, this would be my first time
answers.c: Yes, but something about it didn't feel right
answers.d: I'm currently in therapy and looking for something alongside or instead

pq8.text: What do you hope will change in your life?
pq8.infoText: Optional. Write as little or as much as you like.
pq8.placeholder: Tell us in your own words...

meta.multiSelectHint: Choose more than one if needed
meta.freeTextPlaceholder: Write as much or as little as you want.
meta.gentleReminderBody: You do not need perfect words to continue. A good match often begins with a few honest signals, not a complete explanation.
meta.gentleReminderShort: You can always add more detail later.
meta.gentleReminderLong: You can always switch back to the shorter path.
meta.continueTitle: A good first step should feel more grounded than random.
meta.continueDescription: You can take the quiz again, compare your recommendations once more, or head back to the homepage and continue from there when it feels right.

short.title: A short match, then a clearer next step.
short.description: This version keeps things light and gets you to a thoughtful recommendation quickly.
short.switchDescription: The longer path adds more detail about pace, preferences, and how you want the work to feel.

long.title: A fuller picture for a more precise match.
long.description: This version asks a little more so the recommendation can be more specific.
long.switchCta: Go back to the short version
long.switchDescription: You can always return to the shorter version if you want to move faster.

matching.noResults: We couldn't find a match. Try adjusting some of your preferences.

recommendations.title: Three professionals worth beginning with
recommendations.description: These are not random names from a directory. Each recommendation is tied to the kind of support, pace, and first impression you told us you were hoping for.
recommendations.callDescription: A short first call can help you hear their tone, ask about availability, and decide whether the first session feels right.
